name: Megiddo Tel
description: Megiddo Tel, a strategically vital tell (archaeological mound) in the Jezreel Valley, held immense significance in Canaanite mythology and history. Its location, overlooking key trade routes, made it a constant site of conflict and a powerful symbol of dominion. The city that stood atop Megiddo Tel was believed to be a place where the earthly and divine realms intersected, with temples dedicated to prominent Canaanite deities like Baal and Astarte. Battles fought at Megiddo were not merely territorial disputes but cosmic struggles reflecting the ongoing conflict between order and chaos, fertility and drought, life and death. The city's repeated destruction and rebuilding mirrored the cyclical nature of the seasons and the perpetual struggle for power among the gods. It was a place where kings sought divine favor and where the fates of nations were decided, making it a potent symbol of power, destruction, and resilience in the Canaanite worldview. The fertile valley below, often drenched in blood from countless battles, further reinforced the connection between Megiddo and the themes of sacrifice and renewal. The tel itself became a layered narrative, each stratum representing a different era of divine influence and human endeavor, a testament to the enduring power of the Canaanite deities and the relentless cycle of creation and destruction.
subtitle: Crossroads of Gods and Kings

keyMyths.title: Baal's Victory at Megiddo
keyMyths.description: Legend tells of Baal defending Megiddo against a monstrous serpent, securing the city's prosperity. This victory symbolized Baal's dominion over chaos and his role as protector of the land.

keyMyths.title: The Sacrifice at Megiddo
keyMyths.description: To ensure a bountiful harvest, the king of Megiddo would offer a sacrifice to Astarte at the temple. This ritual was meant to appease the goddess and secure fertility for the land.

sources.author: Mark S. Smith
sources.work: The Ugaritic Baal Cycle, Volume I
sources.description: Provides insight into the character of Baal and Canaanite mythology.
sources.text: Provides insight into the character of Baal and Canaanite mythology.
